items.title: Spurs will be without Carter Bryant vs. Bucks tonight after Corliss Williamson indicated he may be done playing here in Vegas.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Spurs will be without Carter Bryant vs. Bucks tonight after Corliss Williamson indicated he may be done playing here in Vegas. 

Also, no Tarris Reed Jr., who is out for rest after notching 5 points and 9 rebounds (4 on offensive end) in the 70-49 win over New York on Saturday.

items.title: Carter Bryant, Corliss Williamson applaud Wemby for making team-building contract decision.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Carter Bryant, Corliss Williamson applaud Wemby for making team-building contract decision.

"It just says a lot about his character…to be able to sacrifice like that and think about the bigger picture rather than thinking about himself,” Williamson said. 

www.expressnews.com/sports/spurs...

media.title: Carter Bryant praises Victor Wembanyama's contract sacrifice for Spurs
media.description: The forward joined assistant coach Corliss Willaimson in applauding the 22-year-old Frenchman for leaving money on the table to help build the roster around him.

items.title: Corliss Williamson indicated that this likely was Carter Bryant’s last game in Vegas: “As of now, we're talking about it. We started off saying he's going to play two games, so he's played two. Second game was a lot better and we saw what we needed to see.”
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt: Corliss Williamson indicated that this likely was Carter Bryant’s last game in Vegas: “As of now, we're talking about it. We started off saying he's going to play two games, so he's played two. Second game was a lot better and we saw what we needed to see.”

items.title: Carter Bryant with nine points in 2Q after Corliss Williamson said Thursday night he expected the second-year pro to bounce back today. Spurs up 23-12.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt: Carter Bryant with nine points in 2Q after Corliss Williamson said Thursday night he expected the second-year pro to bounce back today. Spurs up 23-12.

items.title: Corliss Williamson said Carter Bryant's only playing time in Summer League will come in Vegas.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Corliss Williamson said Carter Bryant's only playing time in Summer League will come in Vegas.

"It's an opportunity for him to explore his game a little bit...It will be a great opportunity for him to get out there and play."

items.title: Per Summer Spurs coach Corliss Williamson:
items.author: Jeff McDonald
items.body_excerpt:
Per Summer Spurs coach Corliss Williamson:

Jayden Quaintance (knee) will travel with the squad to San Francisco and Las Vegas but is not expected to play.

Carter Bryant is on the summer roster but will not debut until Vegas.

items.title: Corliss Williamson said Jayden Quaintance (knee) will travel with the Summer League squad to San Francisco and Las Vegas but will not play:
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Corliss Williamson said Jayden Quaintance (knee) will travel with the Summer League squad to San Francisco and Las Vegas but will not play:

"He's here at every practice. He's engaged. Good young man. I like his talent and look forward to seeing him for some years."

items.title: Spurs announce assistant Corliss Williamson will coach their Summer League squad in San Francisco and Las Vegas.
items.author: Tom Orsborn
items.body_excerpt:
Spurs announce assistant Corliss Williamson will coach their Summer League squad in San Francisco and Las Vegas.

Carter Bryant heads the team, which includes draft picks Jayden Quaintance, Tarris Reed Jr., Ja'Kobi Gillespie and Maliq Brown.

items.title: 7 years ago, Pelicans ticket office went wild after winning the #1 lottery pick. 7 years later, Zion Williamson has yet to make a playoff debut.
items.author: opkpopfanboyv3
items.body_excerpt: Zion Williamson is prolly the Mr. Potential Man of today. The hype he got before getting drafted was insane. When the Face of the League discussion was still going, I remember seeing a post saying something like "Americans once had someone who they think as the Face of the NBA.…
